name: Web Fetcher
purpose: Bypasses anti-bot measures to fetch, render, and extract clean, LLM-ready markdown or JSON from specific URLs.

safety,cost,resources.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #2 (and Tier <= 2) strictly mandates a Cloud API. Jina AI Reader completely offloads JS rendering to the cloud, guaranteeing absolute isolation from the macOS host.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #3 mandates a Dockerized provider. Crawl4AI runs inside an OrbStack container to provide the required execution boundary for Python-based scraping.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 forbids paid Cloud APIs. With Safety as the secondary priority, Crawl4AI is Dockerized to ensure free local fetching without exposing the bare-metal host.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 forbids paid Cloud APIs. With Resources prioritized over Safety, Crawl4AI is executed bare-metal in the Python .venv to completely bypass OrbStack virtualization overhead.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #3 (and Tier <= 2) strictly mandates a Cloud API. Jina AI Reader ensures zero local compute is consumed for fetching, preserving all 16GB of unified memory.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #2 mandates highly optimized native execution. Crawl4AI runs bare-metal to deliver asynchronous scraping with absolute minimal CPU/RAM impact.

safety,cost,resources.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #2 (and Tier <= 2) strictly mandates a Cloud API. Jina AI Reader offloads execution, ensuring no malformed fetched data can exploit the Windows host.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #3 mandates a Dockerized provider. Crawl4AI leverages WSL2's highly efficient containerization to isolate the fetcher from the Windows filesystem.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 demands a free, locally hosted solution. Safety #2 dictates Dockerization, making WSL2-hosted Crawl4AI the optimal balance of zero financial expenditure and secure containment.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 demands a free solution. Resources #2 dictates bypassing WSL2 overhead where possible. Crawl4AI is executed bare-metal for maximum asynchronous speed on a 16GB Windows machine.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #3 (and Tier <= 2) strictly mandates a Cloud API. This eliminates all local headless browser overhead, keeping the Windows host highly responsive.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #2 mandates highly optimized native execution. Crawl4AI's Python implementation is run bare-metal to maximize efficiency.

safety,cost,resources.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #2 (and Tier <= 2) mandates a Cloud API. Jina AI Reader isolates external web interactions from the 24/7 monolith, ensuring core database services cannot be compromised.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 combined with Cost #3 mandates a Dockerized provider. ScrapeGraphAI provides containerized isolation, while its self-healing LLM logic is critical for maintaining 24/7 background tasks.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 forbids paid APIs. ScrapeGraphAI dynamically adapts to DOM changes, preventing 24/7 fetch failures, while Dockerization satisfies the Safety #2 requirement.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 forbids paid APIs. With Resources #2 over Safety, ScrapeGraphAI is executed bare-metal to eliminate Docker network overhead while maintaining high resilience for background tasks.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #3 (and Tier <= 2) strictly mandates a Cloud API. This preserves the 32GB+ monolith's CPU/RAM entirely for VictoriaLogs and PostgreSQL vector indexing.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 combined with Cost #2 strictly mandates optimized native execution. ScrapeGraphAI runs bare-metal to ensure the background fetching daemon consumes minimal continuous resources.

safety,cost,resources.why: Safety #1 forbids bare-metal. Because this is Tier > 2, Cloud APIs are strictly forbidden. The rules therefore mandate a Dockerized deployment. Firecrawl securely contains heavy Redis/Postgres workers in isolated containers on the blast-zone.

safety,resources,cost.why: Safety #1 forbids bare-metal. Because this is Tier > 2, Cloud APIs are strictly forbidden. The rules therefore mandate a Dockerized deployment. Firecrawl securely contains heavy Redis/Postgres workers in isolated containers on the blast-zone.

cost,safety,resources.why: Cost #1 demands a free local solution. Safety #2 mandates containerization. Firecrawl's self-hosted architecture is deployed inside Docker to securely contain the blast radius of heavy, concurrent headless browsers.

cost,resources,safety.why: Cost #1 demands a free solution. Resources #2 dictates bypassing container overhead. Crawl4AI running natively bare-metal maximizes raw asynchronous Python throughput directly on the blast-zone CPU.

resources,safety,cost.why: Resources #1 demands minimal host impact. Because Tier > 2 strictly forbids Cloud APIs, we must bypass heavy Docker VMs and fall back to highly optimized native binaries. Crawl4AI runs bare-metal to maximize efficiency.

resources,cost,safety.why: Resources #1 demands minimal host impact. Because Tier > 2 strictly forbids Cloud APIs, we must bypass heavy Docker VMs and fall back to highly optimized native binaries. Crawl4AI runs bare-metal to maximize efficiency.
